## [03:00 - 06:02]

because their existing webinar and its funnel was created for their existing warm audience. And they do that either consciously or unconsciously. And so both the webinar and its funnel don't contain enough trust building elements to make it consistently profitable for cold traffic. So I want to make sure that you really understand what I just said. So the problem with the vast majority of webinars when we try to make them go evergreen is that there aren't enough trust elements built into the webinar and the funnel around the webinar to convince people who don't know who you are, in other words, cold traffic, that they should trust you enough to hand you money. That's the single biggest problem in most evergreen webinar setup. And it happens, as I said here on this screen, it happens either unconsciously or unconsciously because we tend to build webinars for our warm traffic audience who naturally already trust us. And so we build it, we put in trust elements and those things, but those trust elements work because these people already trust us. And so when we try to apply this same funnel to people who don't trust us yet, it fails because we don't have enough trust elements built into it, which brings up the bonus tip. Oftentimes, the best way to save a suffering evergreen webinar is to add more trust elements into the funnel, which usually involves putting more trust elements before asking people to register for the webinar. So in other words, warming up that cold traffic before you make an offer to them.
